perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Term This agreement is for a term of ten (10) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Term This agreement is for a term of five (5) years.
perturbation.explanation: The initial contract specifies a ten-year term for the agreement. This change modifies the term to five years, creating an immediate contradiction. This directly affects section 13.2 as it conflicts with the exclusive rights granted for ten years, therefore creating uncertainty and potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2 The Manufacturer grants exclusive rights to the Customer for the term of ten (10) years from the date of the signing of this agreement and for an indefinite period upon the customer fulfilling the minimum annual purchase requirement as listed in Schedule B. of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the contract term from ten (10) years to five (5) years. The contradicted text grants exclusive rights for a term of ten (10) years. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the exclusive rights, as the term of the agreement itself has been shortened, therefore the term of the rights becomes conflicting and uncertain. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction.
